observedSectionWindows.note: Opening title, scope, applicability, and early definitions.
Core requirements, control language, and middle-file governance / reporting material.
Closing requirements and the end-of-source boundary.

boundaries: Keep Regulation 213 focused on the core regulation file only.
Keep the amendment / FAQ PDFs in the same folder out of this wave unless a later amendment-history plan explicitly opens them.
Preserve page locators and line references where available, but expect the OCR layer to remain noisy and page-image-backed.
Keep the opening, middle, and closing windows separate so the boundary logic stays easy to review.
exclusions: No eLearning scripts, slides, quizzes, or app exports.
No raw source files in Git.
No amendment-history files in the same wave.
No practice-note files in the same wave.

reviewStandards.stateRegulation: Treat the source as jurisdiction-specific regulatory text, not as a companion note.
reviewStandards.jurisdictionSpecificRequirement: Treat New York-specific requirements as jurisdiction-specific requirements when they direct conduct or filing.
reviewStandards.regulatoryRequirement: Treat direct obligations, prohibitions, filing rules, and control requirements as regulatory text.
reviewStandards.definitionOrTerminology: Treat defined terms and regulatory labels as terminology when the text explains rather than directs.
reviewStandards.governanceOrControlExpectation: Treat approval, oversight, control, and responsible-actuary language as governance or control expectations when they appear.
reviewStandards.reportingRequirement: Treat filing, submission, and notice language as reporting requirements if they appear.
reviewStandards.documentationExpectation: Treat retention, memorandum, and recordkeeping language as documentation expectations if they appear.
reviewStandards.crossReferenceMapping: Treat references to other regulations or statutes as mapping context unless the text operationalizes the reference.
reviewStandards.backgroundContent: Treat title and heading material as background unless the text imposes a duty.
reviewStandards.boundaryControlWindow: Treat each page window as review-only and do not widen it unless later cleanup explicitly needs that split.
reviewStandards.requiresHumanInterpretation: Treat dense or noisy regulatory wording as requiring human review unless the page image confirms the wording.

currentChecksNeeded: Plan file exists and parses as JSON.
The Reg-213 plan markdown must exist and carry the expected headings.
The Reg-213 batch-definition file must exist and stay synchronized with the plan.
Every planned batch must remain review-only by default.
The source posture must stay explicitly active.
The OCR layer should remain page-image-backed and the plan should mention that line references are not expected.
The amendment / FAQ files in the same folder must stay out of this wave.
futureChecksSuggested: After the wave completes, add review-index and self-review checks plus a proof-of-concept summary reference.
Keep the plan portable if a later amendment-history wave is approved.

operatingNote.sourceType: text-based PDF source with noisy OCR
operatingNote.citationGuidance: Page locators are the primary anchor and line references are not expected to be stable for this wave.
operatingNote.boundaryGuidance: The active regulation text should remain visible as a review-only caveat rather than a promotion signal.
operatingNote.appendixGuidance: Keep the amendment and FAQ PDFs in the same folder out of this wave.
